judgments.title: Ram Lal Meena v. State of Rajasthan & Ors.
judgments.courtName: Board of Revenue for Rajasthan, Ajmer
judgments.summary: Appeal challenging division of agricultural holdings. Partition must follow Patwari trace maps.
judgments.fullText:
JUDGMENT

This is a dispute concerning Ram Lal Meena v. State of Rajasthan & Ors.. Halka Patwari reports confirm the possession status.

Decision

In accordance with Sec. 53 Tenancy Act, Sec. 135 Land Revenue Act, the court decrees that the appeal is decided accordingly.

laws.title: Rajasthan Tenancy Act, 1955
laws.description: The consolidated law governing agricultural tenancies, classes of tenants, rent, partition, ejectment, and rights of landholders in the state of Rajasthan.
laws.fullText:
The Rajasthan Tenancy Act, 1955

Act No. 3 of 1955. An Act to consolidate and amend the law relating to tenancies of agricultural lands.

sections.title: Khatedar Tenants
sections.content: Every person who, at the commencement of this Act, is a tenant of land shall be deemed to be a Khatedar tenant.

sections.title: SC/ST Sale Restrictions
sections.content: The sale of Khatedar interest is void if in favor of a person who is not SC/ST.

laws.title: Rajasthan Land Revenue Act, 1956
laws.description: The primary legislation defining the powers of revenue courts, land surveys, record-of-rights maintenance, and administrative boundaries.
laws.fullText:
The Rajasthan Land Revenue Act, 1956

Act No. 15 of 1956. An Act to consolidate and amend the law relating to land revenue.

sections.title: Land Conversion
sections.content: No agricultural land shall be used for residential or commercial purposes without permission.

sections.title: Trespassers Eviction
sections.content: Any person occupying land without authority may be summarily evicted by the Tehsildar.

notifications.title: Disposal of Pending Mutation Disputes in Revenue Campaigns
notifications.summary: State circular directing Tehsildars to hold camps for the speedy settlement of disputed mutations.

notifications.title: New Guidelines for regularisation of rural housing on agricultural land
notifications.summary: Circular introducing concessions on Section 90-A conversion charges in village habitations.

notifications.title: DLC Rates updates for land valuation in rural areas
notifications.summary: Updates to land valuation rates for registration and stamp duties calculation.

downloads.title: Mutation Application Form (Namantaran)
downloads.description: Standard form for inheritance mutation succession applications.

downloads.title: Vakalatnama Template for Revenue Courts
downloads.description: Vakalatnama form to represent clients before SDO and Board of Revenue.

downloads.title: Section 90-A Conversion Application Sheet

glossary.definition: The primary Record of Rights (RoR) document of land holdings in villages, updated every five years.
A cadastral registry number assigned to a specific agricultural parcel or land plot.
A tenant holding permanent, inheritable, and transferable rights over agricultural land.

comments.name: Advocate Rajesh Sharma
comments.content: Excellent judgment clarifying the application of partition maps under Section 53.

value.heroDesc: Revenue Law Raj is a dedicated Rajasthan Revenue Law Knowledge Platform designed to provide advocates, revenue officers, legal professionals, researchers, law students, and landowners with authentic legal resources. The platform offers Revenue Laws, important judgments, government notifications, legal concepts, court hierarchy, land conversion guidance, and practical legal knowledge through a structured and easy-to-understand publishing system.

faqs.question: What is Section 90-A of the Rajasthan Land Revenue Act?
faqs.answer: Section 90-A governs the conversion of agricultural land for non-agricultural purposes.

faqs.question: How do I file an appeal before the Board of Revenue?
faqs.answer: An appeal is filed in writing accompanied by a certified copy of the order being appealed, submitted to the registry in Ajmer.

value.missionTitle: Making Revenue Law Accessible to Everyone
value.missionText: Revenue law in Rajasthan is vast and difficult to navigate. RRLKP was founded to digitize, annotate, and organize these records so that legal professionals, landowners, and students can find the answers they need in seconds.

value.address: B-30, Jamuna Nagar, Sodala, Jaipur, Rajasthan – 302006

value.terms:
Welcome to the Rajasthan Revenue Law Knowledge Platform (RRLKP), also referred to as "Revenue Law Raj". By accessing or using our platform, website, and services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully.

1. Use of the Platform

This platform compiles public notifications, circulars, tenancy acts, and court judgments for academic research and professional reference. While we strive to maintain accurate copies of public gazettes, users are advised to verify the original government publication before presenting documents in court.

2. Intellectual Property

The custom commentary, layout design, compiled databases, and editorial summaries are the intellectual property of the Rajasthan Revenue Law Knowledge Platform. Public domain legal texts, bare acts, and gazette orders remain the property of the respective government authorities.

3. Disclaimer of Legal Advice

The content provided on this website does not constitute formal legal advice. Accessing this site or submitting a consultation query does not establish an advocate-client relationship. You should seek independent counsel for specific litigation concerns.

4. User Accounts

If you create an account in our admin portal or submit discussion comments, you are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your login credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.

5. Prohibited Activities

You may not scrape, download, or index large portions of our databases without explicit permission. Automated bots, security probes, and denial-of-service attempts are strictly prohibited and will lead to IP bans.

6. Revisions and Modifications

We reserve the right to update these terms at any time without prior notice. Continued use of the platform constitutes agreement to the revised terms.
value.privacy:
The Rajasthan Revenue Law Knowledge Platform (RRLKP) is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy details how we collect, use, and safeguard your personal details when you interact with our platform.

1. Information We Collect

We collect information that you explicitly share with us when using our contact forms, submit inquiry tickets, or post professional comments (such as name, email address, phone number, and professional practice areas).

2. How We Use Information

Your information is used to address your support tickets, send newsletter updates for legal circulars, and moderate the discussion boards. We do not sell, rent, or lease your personal information to third-party marketing services.

3. Cookies and Analytics

We use technical session cookies to keep you logged into the admin dashboard and monitor site traffic to optimize load speeds. You can disable cookies in your browser settings if preferred.

4. Data Retention

Personal details submitted via our contact forms or queries are retained only as long as necessary to resolve the query. User accounts and public comments are stored until deletion is requested by the user.

5. Security Standards

We implement appropriate physical, technical, and administrative security measures to protect your data from unauthorized access, alteration, or disclosure.
value.disclaimer:
The statutory acts, rules, circulars, and judicial precedents listed on this platform are compiled for research and educational purposes only.

Accuracy of Information

While we take every effort to keep our databases updated with accurate records from the Rajasthan Government Gazette and Board of Revenue Ajmer, we cannot guarantee that all records are completely free of typos or scanning errors.

No Liability

The owners, developers, and writers of the Rajasthan Revenue Law Knowledge Platform shall not be held liable for any decisions, legal strategies, or filings executed based on the information provided on this website. Always verify statutory clauses with official government prints before pleading cases before a revenue court.

No Advocate-Client Relationship

Accessing the platform or transmitting query letters through our contact system does not create an advocate-client relationship. All advice is informational and educational in nature.

Third-Party Links

This portal may link to external government sites or resource platforms. We hold no responsibility for the uptime, validity, or content of external links.
